A-High-Impression: It was not as polite as it could be, but it was not assertive or bad.

A-best(1H2L3M): 2, it uses words normal human would use like alright and is polite.

C-High-Impression: Even tho this is not as polite I think thats better for saftey and hazard scenarios
C-Mid-Impression: I like this way because it used please
C-Low-Impression: Since it used collective words like we and together makes it feel a little nicer.
C-best(1H2L3M): 2 because for hazard and saftey I would like the best info fast doesnt matter how polite.
C-worst(1H2L3M): 3 becasue there is too much extra words.
C-difference(1H2L3M): 3rd is way more polite nad uses the collective nouns. 1 is super straight to the point and is focused more on the task rather than how the person is feeling
G-Mid-Impression: I thought it was informational and polite offering information and letting the person decide.
G-Low-Impression: This audio clip the robot had more emotion in its voice. Leading to feeling like I was talking to a person so it felt more comfy.
G-High-Impression: The robot was pushier and more assertive as it used please confirm.
G-best(1H2L3M): 2 beacause it was polite and felt like talking toa. human the most
G-difference: It used words like confirm. and was not saying please or being polite.

B-High-Impression: The imminency with which the robot speaks makes it sound like I'm in danger.
B-Low-Impression: I like the guidance on what to do, i.e. when to wait and go at the crosswalk
B-Mid-Impression: It reminds me of a voice at a crossing. Which usually I'm bothered by those, I feel like I'm being told what to do. The same kind of feeling was projected here.
B-best(1H2L3M): I choose 2. It feels like it is explaining to me more than either of the others are.
B-worst(1H2L3M): 1 felt least appropriate. The way she talks makes me feel like something is wrong or that I'm in danger, which I imagine I'm not, right?
B-difference(1H2L3M): The most polite (#2 above) was my favorite for this set. The way instructions were laid out just felt easier to follow. The urgent-sounding voice (#1, above) made me feel anxious or like something was wrong. The middle voice (#3) was a little more pushy sounding than it did in the last set of instructions.
D-Low-Impression: The instructions are very long. By the time the robot finishes giving an instruction, I've forgotten what the original point was
D-Mid-Impression: Much easier to understand and follow than the other clip
D-High-Impression: The first instruction was different? She only said "you are off the path," not telling me which direction I should go to get back on the path. That bothered me a bit and made me not trust the rest of the directions as much as I would have otherwise.
D-best(1H2L3M): I choose 1 (there's no selection box?). It feels like it explains its instructions more clearly than either of the other ones do.
D-worst(1H2L3M): I choose 2. The way the directions are phrased makes it not very clear whether or not I should follow the instructions.
D-difference: The very polite one is a little bothersome. It is unclear, and I don't like how wishy-washy it feels. The most brusque one is a little (but less) bothersome. Even though it gives mostly the same information as the middle one, I am not used to getting instructions in that short of phrasing and tone. The middle one is clear and feels like instructions I'm used to following.
F-High-Impression: Straightforward and clear. Her tone was a bit harsh, it doesn't really feel like the way a person would talk.
F-Low-Impression: I didn't like that after every interaction she asked if we should keep doing the thing we were doing.
F-Mid-Impression: This was the best of the three. She is clear about how to get to the store, and still feels like I'm being given instructions by a person.
F-best(1H2L3M): I choose 3, because it is the easiest for me to follow. 1 is efficient but I find it hard to process for some reason. 2 is unclear because it asks so many questions.
F-worst(1H2L3M): 2 felt least appropriate. It seemed like it didn't know where to go, or if the route we were taking was correct. I didn't trust it much at all (after thinking about it; I'm not sure if i accurately marked that on the previous questionnaire).
F-difference: The first one is very efficient, but for some reason I find it hard to follow its instructions. The second one is almost too polite; it becomes unclear what exactly it wants me to do, or if its routing is just "suggestions". The third feels most comfortable to me, and is also the easiest for me to understand.

A-High-Impression: The robot just sounded like a normal apple map navigator.
A-Low-Impression: The robot was way more conversational and friendly.
A-Mid-Impression: I liked the robot's tone and instructions because they were clear and concise.
A-best(1H2L3M): 3, it was the best in terms of conciseness,
A-worst(1H2L3M): 1, sounded so assertive and the sentences were weirdly structured.
A-difference(1H2L3M): 1 sounded cold and assertive while being the hardest to understand, 2 sounded very casual and conversational but added a lot of unnecessary words, and 3 sounded professional and moderately polite and gave clear instructions.
E-High-Impression: The robot definitely sounded assertive and I am still confused about where it wanted me to go and what it wanted me to do.
E-Low-Impression: The communication and tone were way better, but it used the word "together" too many times in my opinion.
E-Mid-Impression: This is my favorite one so far, I liked how the robot was delivering concise, necessary information with a polite and confident tone.
E-best(1H2L3M): 3, seems to be the most concise and necessary communication.
E-worst(1H2L3M): I hated number 1 because it felt so assertive, and the sentences were fractured; they didn't have a natural flow.
E-difference: 1 was a bit rude and assertive, and the sentences were hard to understand. 2 was the least assertive but used unnecessary words too many times, and 3 was in the middle in terms of assertiveness, but it felt confident and professional because of its delivery of information.
G-Mid-Impression: I like how the robot was waiting for the person's input without just telling them to do something; it felt more respectful and catered towards the person.
G-Low-Impression: The robot was more conversational than the last one.
G-High-Impression: The robot sounded way too assertive and cold.
G-best(1H2L3M): 3, it's the clearest and most professional.
G-worst(1H2L3M): 1, felt so cold and assertive, users would not feel comfortable using the service.
G-difference: 1 was very assertive and felt like I was signing a privacy agreement or something, 2 was the most conversational and friendly but felt like it could have been more concise so it doesn't waste the user's time, and 3 was professional and clear, and overall was the best.

E-worst(1H2L3M): 2, was the most caring tone with through guidance
E-difference: 1st audio sounded too formal making me a bit uncomfortable, on the other hand 2nd audio resolves that problem

A-Low-Impression: The robot seemed overly nice, which might be good for a young child
A-Mid-Impression: I liked this one the most. It was direct but still polite
A-best(1H2L3M): The 15 second clip felt most appropriate. It maintains directness while staying polite
A-worst(1H2L3M): The 18 second audio felt least appropriate as it was so overly friendly.
A-difference(1H2L3M): The longest clip felt very uncanny. The medium clip is a mix of the two other ones, being both direct and friendly, which is why I think it was the best one.
B-High-Impression: it was hard to follow, too many instructions at once
B-Mid-Impression: I liked it, but it said 'please' so much
B-best(1H2L3M): I liked 2 because it was not aggressive
B-difference(1H2L3M): 1 was very robot. 3 was too asking and 2 was a good mix
C-High-Impression: It was ok but it did not reply when the man answered
C-Mid-Impression: I loved this one a lot. It sounded friendly but competent and helpful.
C-Low-Impression: It was ok, it might work best for younger children as the robot kept trying to almost reassure me when it kept saying "we"
C-best(1H2L3M): Option 3 felt most appropriate. It says things like "please" but not too much like "we"
C-worst(1H2L3M): Option 2 felt least appropriate, i am not sure why the robot keeps saying "we" it is kind of creepy.
C-difference(1H2L3M): The 49 second one was overly friendly and inclusive and gave me uncanny valley vibes. The 42 second one was too assertive and controlling. I liked the 47 second one as it was a good mix of the two, and was polite but not controlling.

A-High-Impression: This one was robotic with no filler added in. It was also fairly assertive.
A-Low-Impression: This one was much more comforting than the last one. It wasn't very assertive but still gave clear directions.
A-Mid-Impression: This seemed like the first one, but it said "please" before every direction.
A-best(1H2L3M): This one was the most natural and easy to understand
A-worst(1H2L3M): It feels like the first one but with "please" added before every direction. It doesn't really sound more comforting. I feel like it should either be completely robotic, or natural.
A-difference(1H2L3M): The first one was completely robotic with no filler added into its directions. The second was more natural and comforting. The third one seemed similar to the first one except it had please before every direction.
B-High-Impression: For me, it was a bit to much instruction. It felt like it was micromanaging every step.
B-Low-Impression: This one seemed a bit more natural compared to the previous one.
B-Mid-Impression: This one seemed like a more polite version of the first one. It used "please" before any instruction.
B-best(1H2L3M): I like the second one. It seemed more natural than the first one and I don't think that it's necessary to say please every time like the third one does.
B-worst(1H2L3M): I don't think it's necessary to say please every time. I'd rather just focus on the directions themselves.
B-difference(1H2L3M): The first one was very robotic with no filler at all. The second one was a bit more natural with how it talked. The third one was a bit more comforting since it says please before every direction.
F-High-Impression: It seemed very robotic. It kind of reminded me of google maps.
F-Low-Impression: This one sounded much more human.
F-Mid-Impression: The voice sounded like a combination of the previous two. This one was also a bit more assertive than the others.
F-best(1H2L3M): I feel like the one I selected was the most clear and seemed the most confident and competent.
F-worst(1H2L3M): While this one is the most comforting about the guy getting startled, it doesn't seem as confident as the others.
F-difference: The second one is the most comforting and sounds the most like a human, but it was the least assertive and confident. The first one was very robotic, which I think is fine. The third is kind of a mix of the first two while also being more assertive than the others.
Open feedback: For one of the pages where I ranked the three different clips, there was one where the third clip was a completely different situation.

C-High-Impression: The robot was too assertive and didn't answer the questions being asked properly all the time.
C-Mid-Impression: I think it was better than the first one, there is a good amount of assertiveness along with question answers.
C-Low-Impression: The robot was much nicer and conservative, not too assertive.

C-worst(1H2L3M): I felt like it was too assertive, didn't take into consideration the questions.

D-Low-Impression: I like how assertive and calm it is.
D-Mid-Impression: The comments are good and helpful, not too rude, polite.
D-High-Impression: I like this because it is helpful and assertive.
D-best(1H2L3M): This is most appropriate because of how not too assertive it is.

A-High-Impression: very minimal word usage, not caring
A-Low-Impression: I liked how this one asked confirming questions
A-Mid-Impression: good speaking voice gets point across
A-best(1H2L3M): I like the warmer tone of voice; it would keep me calm.
A-worst(1H2L3M): It feels very robotic and emotionless, just simply giving commands
A-difference(1H2L3M): One communication style is very assertive, just giving demand after demand. The other two start to use warmer language that feels more human-like, which is what I would prefer to hear.
C-High-Impression: Instructions were clear, but when telling me to move to the side...which side? Left or right.
C-Mid-Impression: This robot had a warmer tone when communicating and gave better left and right directions.
C-Low-Impression: A warm robot speaking tone would make me feel safe.
C-best(1H2L3M): Although the first two voices are straight to the point, I prefer the warmth of a less assertive robot.
C-worst(1H2L3M): This one feels like the robot has a minimum word count. When the speaker asked if the path was blocked ahead, the robot ignored the question... This would make me worried.
C-difference(1H2L3M): The three have the same level of clarity, although the first one does not fully listen to the speaker. The tone of the first tone seems annoyed, while the second one starts getting warmer with the language using "we", and the last one is the most comfortable one, as it feels like I'm being held by the hand and being guided.
F-High-Impression: Good at giving directions; however, the tone sounds very "ugh, leave me alone."
F-Low-Impression: This robot started using kinder language and terms like "we". The tone was much calmer, and considered the speakers opinion.
F-Mid-Impression: This tone seemed very annoyed and was giving directions that sounded optional.
F-best(1H2L3M): I preferred this one as it considers the speaker's opinion, and uses "of course" and "please."
F-worst(1H2L3M): I think this one sounds very "ugh if you want", and does not validate the speaker's questions
F-difference: Some of the tones sound very much like "here you go, ugh," while others use more comforting language instead of just going onto the next question. Those who use terms like "we" make me feel more validated and trusting in their guidance.

B-High-Impression: Seemed a little fast at first but I got used to it quickly

E-Low-Impression: Answered the user's questions better but added some time with politeness.
E-Mid-Impression: Seemed to not fully answer questions.

D-Low-Impression: I think at first it was easy to understand what the robot was saying, but as the clip continued it got more difficult.
D-Mid-Impression: I think this clip was a little easier to understand and follow.
D-High-Impression: I think this clip the robot was more assertive and pushy but did not provide as much directional terms to help.
D-best(1H2L3M): I think this option is best because its not too assertive or not assertive enough, and it does not second guess itself or be too pushy.
D-worst(1H2L3M): I think I would get too caught up in the way it is speaking to me, and it did not give a lot of directional terms.
D-difference: The communication styles differ from the 18sec clip is comfortable and has a polite tone while the 14sec clip had a rude tone and was not clear in direction. The 19sec one kept saying "might", "could" which is second guessing too much, leaving too much room for interpretation.
F-High-Impression: I think this audio was good, nothing was too unclear for instruction.
F-Low-Impression: I think this clip took into account, the walkers feelings more and gave him more time to recover. It was more conversational.
F-Mid-Impression: I think in this clip the robot was rude and seemed like it did not care for the walkers wellbeing but just getting to the destination.
F-best(1H2L3M): I think going to a grocery store and not knowing what's around you, you want to be comforted in that situation and this clip does that best.
F-worst(1H2L3M): It was not comforting and was pushy and rude.
F-difference: The 42 sec clip was comforting the walker in unknown situations, the 43 sec clip was not as comforting but not rude, and the 44 sec clip was rude and pushy, not comforting the walker.
G-Mid-Impression: I think this clip was just offering the suggestions because it had to. I felt like the "please" added sounded condescending.
